decision: Reopened closed PR #474 (branch codex/herdr-claude-ai-coding) to address its 4 Copilot review comments and, per operator directive, verify the Herdr+Claude AI-coding option actually works end-to-end. Fixed: (1) AGENT_ICONS/AGENT_ACCENTS 'single source of truth' comment was aspirational — app/ai-coding/page.tsx AI_TOOLS and AgentTabsLayout.tsx TOOL_META each inlined their own duplicate icon/accent literals; both now import and read from components/icons/AgentIcons.tsx so all three consumers (tree, tabs, settings) stay in sync. (2) page.tsx header comment claimed all icons are brand marks, but herdr-claude used a generic Lucide Terminal icon; now sources from AGENT_ICONS (which uses the same Lucide SquareTerminal fallback) and the comment states Herdr has no embeddable brand mark. (3) Reworded the AgentIcons.tsx module header so the 'monochrome, single-path glyph' claim explicitly scopes to the five named brand-mark components, not the whole module (closes the suppressed low-confidence Copilot comment re: SquareTerminal being stroke-based). (4) server/handlers/command-handler.ts: the Herdr server-ready wait loop always exited 0 (last statement is `break` or `sleep`), masking a startup timeout; added an explicit post-loop status re-check that bails into an interactive shell with the server log on failure. (5) The `herdr agent start claude ... || { cat log || true; }` fallback swallowed failures and fell through to `exec herdr --session daax` with no agent running; now bails into a shell with the log printed instead of continuing.
criticalBugFound: Independent review (advisor) flagged that the array-of-strings `.join(" && ")` construction produced `herdr server ... & && for i in ...` — a backgrounded command (`&`) directly followed by the `&&` join separator is a shell syntax error in both bash and zsh (verified via `bash -n`/`zsh -n`), meaning the entire herdr-claude command had never actually executed through the daax terminal path (pre-existing bug carried over from the original merged/deployed PR, now in scope because the operator required the feature to 'actually work'). Fixed by merging `herdr server ... &` and the poll loop into a single array element (`cmd & for ...; done`) so no `&&` sits directly after the bare `&`; `&` correctly backgrounds the whole preceding and_or chain (export+check+herdr-server) as one job, then the poll loop / re-check / workspace / agent-start / exec run in the foreground.
verification: bash -n and zsh -n confirm valid syntax on the generated command string. Ran the actual generated command against a stub `herdr` binary on PATH exercising all 3 branches: (a) success path — server comes up, workspace+agent start, attaches via `exec herdr --session daax`; (b) server-timeout path — status never reports running, prints server log, bails; (c) agent-start-failure path — herdr agent start exits 1, prints claude log, bails. All 3 behaved as intended (exit codes 0/42/43 respectively, confirmed no fallthrough to the exec-attach step on failure).

scope: Did not touch pre-existing unrelated formatting drift (lib/host-docker.ts, server/handlers/connection-handler.ts, and 4 test files) surfaced by `bun run format:write` — confirmed via `git diff main...HEAD` that none of those files are part of this PR's diff; reverted the accidental reformat.
validation: Producer: Claude Sonnet 5. Validator: advisor (stronger reviewer, same-provider) — caught the critical shell-syntax bug pre-push; cross-provider (Codex) unavailable on this account per prior finding (all models rejected).
nextSteps: Push to codex/herdr-claude-ai-coding, open a new PR (old PR #474 stays closed per repo convention: never update, always recreate), request Copilot review, and iterate to a zero-comment verdict before merge.

decision: PR #475 (recreation of #474) got a fresh Copilot review with 4 new comments, all addressed on the same branch: (1) tests/server/handlers/command-handler.test.ts - added a regression test asserting the generated command never contains a bare `&` directly before `&&` (locks in the shell-syntax fix). (2) app/ai-coding/AgentTabsLayout.tsx - removed its duplicate local `AIToolId` union; now imports and re-exports the canonical type from components/terminal/TerminalManager, dropping the now-unneeded `as ManagerAIToolId` casts at the two createAISession call sites. (3) server/handlers/command-handler.ts - added a comment explaining why `herdr workspace create` uses `--no-focus` (the PR description says 'create/focus the workspace', but the actual focus happens via `--focus` on the agent-start step, once Claude is actually running there; documented intentional behavior rather than changing already-verified-working command flow). (4) app/settings/page.tsx - converted `AI_AGENT_LABELS` from `Record<string,string>` to a `satisfies Record<keyof typeof AGENT_ICONS, string>` literal, matching the self-validating pattern already used by AGENT_ICONS/AGENT_ACCENTS; updated the one indexing call site to narrow via `keyof typeof AI_AGENT_LABELS`.

decision: PR #476 (recreation of #475) got a fresh Copilot review with 1 comment (down from 4): components/terminal/TerminalManager.tsx claimed AIToolId (imported as a type by app/ai-coding/page.tsx and AgentTabsLayout.tsx) was 'not exported' from that module. Verified this was a false positive - grep confirmed `export type { SessionType, AIToolId, AISession };` already existed at the bottom of the file, and `bun run typecheck` passed with 0 errors both before and after, which would be impossible if the type were genuinely unexported and consumed elsewhere. Addressed anyway for clarity/to close the comment: moved the export inline to each type's declaration site (`export type SessionType`, `export type AIToolId`, `export interface AISession`) and removed the now-redundant blanket `export type {...}` re-export at the bottom of the file. No behavior change; purely a legibility fix that removes the ambiguity Copilot's static analysis apparently tripped on.

gates.lint: 0 new issues (2 pre-existing unrelated warnings in TerminalManager.tsx untouched by this diff)

nextSteps: Close PR #476, open a fresh PR (#477) on the same branch, request Copilot review, and confirm zero-comment verdict + green CI + mergeable before considering the goal satisfied.

decision: PR #477 (recreation of #476) got a fresh Copilot review with 1 real comment: server/handlers/command-handler.ts line 16-19 checked `command === "herdr-claude" || command.startsWith("herdr-claude ")` (literal single space) while the arg-stripping line used `/^herdr-claude\s*/` (any whitespace) - a tab-separated invocation like `herdr-claude\t--flag` would skip the special-case branch entirely and run as a raw passthrough command. Confirmed this exact double-check pattern (`===` OR literal-space `startsWith`) pre-exists identically for the `claude` and `opencode` branches in the same file, so it is not herdr-claude-specific, but fixed only the herdr-claude occurrence (in-scope for this PR) by replacing the two checks with a single regex `/^herdr-claude(?:\s|$)/.test(command)`, matching the word-boundary style already used later in the same file for copilot/gemini/codex. Added 2 tests: one confirming a tab-separated invocation is still handled, one confirming `herdr-claude-test` still falls through unchanged (word-boundary regression, mirroring the existing copilot/gemini/codex tests).

All CI checks green: Container image build PASS (4m21s), Trivy fs scan PASS, Lint/typecheck/test/build/auth-audit PASS (3m15s), E2E conditionally skipped. mergeStateStatus=CLEAN, mergeable=MERGEABLE.
arc: Fixed the original 4 Copilot comments from #474 PLUS a critical shell-syntax bug the review missed (`herdr server & && ...` was invalid in bash+zsh, so the feature had literally never run through the daax terminal path). Iterated through recreations #475 (4 more comments) -> #476 (1 false-positive, addressed anyway) -> #477 (1 real whitespace-separator bug) -> #478 (zero comments). 6 total real fixes + 1 false positive across the arc; each recreation per repo convention (Copilot won't re-review an updated PR).
feature_verification: Herdr+Claude AI-coding option: starts `herdr server`, waits for readiness (with explicit fail-closed re-check), creates the mounted workspace, starts Claude as a focused Herdr agent, attaches via `exec herdr --session daax`; Herdr's session model allows starting additional agents once attached. Verified end-to-end against a stub `herdr` binary through success/timeout/agent-failure paths, plus bash -n/zsh -n syntax validation.
note: Operator merges (never the agent). PR left open and mergeable for operator review.
